summary: Full backend+frontend test of CRM Reach Outs + Contact Logs (iteration_72). Backend 16/16 pytest passing — verified route ordering (/leads/reach-outs before /leads/{id}), grouping math (overdue/today/tomorrow/this_week/future>7-day excluded), admin assigned_to filter, contact-log POST/GET, method+outcome enum validation, last_contacted_at stamping, 'contact_logged' activity timeline entry, and stage_changed_at regression on PUT. Frontend verified end-to-end via Playwright with admin mock session: sidebar 'Reach Outs' nav, rep filter (admin), grouped lists rendered, Log button opens LogContactModal, modal saves and closes, list refreshes; Leads section inline MessageSquarePlus button opens modal; Lead Detail modal shows 'Log Contact' button + 'Contact History' panel with method/outcome badges, reply text, and performed_by/timestamp; Activity Timeline shows the new 'Contact logged: ...' entry. All required data-testids confirmed present.
action_items: No fixes required — feature works end-to-end. Optional polish: 'Contact History' panel is conditional on contactLogs.length > 0, so the data-testid 'lead-detail-contact-history' is not rendered until at least one log exists. If contract requires always-rendered placeholder, render an empty-state wrapper with the testid.
critical_code_review_comments: Route ordering correct: GET /leads/reach-outs defined at line 843 — BEFORE /leads/{lead_id} at line 953. Verified via 200 (not 404) without any lead named 'reach-outs'.
Grouping uses date math (due_date - today).days; bucketing logic matches spec: <0 overdue, 0 today, 1 tomorrow, 2..7 this_week, >7 ignored.
Contact log POST validates method+outcome via explicit sets (raises 400, not 422-from-pydantic-enum). Pydantic model still returns 422 for missing/null lead_id which is acceptable.
last_contacted_at + (optional) next_follow_up_date are atomically $set on the lead inside POST /contact-logs. updated_at also stamped — good.
GET /contact-logs scopes to rep's leads via $in query when non-admin — solid.
LogContactModal handles both lead.id and lead.lead_id shapes (defensive). Reasonable.
Pre-existing minor: GET /crm/funnels returns {funnels: [...]} (dict) — frontend handles it, but worth being aware for any new tests.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all required data-testids found, all flows verified)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_iter72 workflow rule + TEST_OverdueLead + TEST_DueTodayLead with stage_changed_at directly stamped in Mongo (gradnext.crm_leads); all cleaned up after run (reach-outs totals returned to 0).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run pytest with env: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<preview-url> MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017 DB_NAME=gradnext. .env values have surrounding quotes (use grep+cut without strip, then trim). /api/crm/funnels returns {funnels:[...]}, not raw list. lead-detail-contact-history only renders when the lead has >=1 contact log; create one first or assert via lead-detail-log-contact-btn presence + the inline LogContactModal selectors.
